headline: Aster's first community beta
summary: Canvas, Clips, Shelf, Bar, Switch, and Keys now work together as a private utility layer for your Mac.

features.title: A thoughtful first run
features.description: Aster introduces itself, explains its privacy promise, and lets everyone choose their starting modules.

features.title: Help where it belongs
features.description: Each module offers a concise guide the first time it is opened, including shortcuts and privacy details.

features.title: Shortcuts and app hotkeys
features.description: Keys includes a Mac shortcut reference, shortcuts that can be edited or disabled, per-item restore, and global hotkeys that open or focus your apps.

features.title: System-wide Now Playing
features.description: Shelf follows the active macOS media session from music apps, video players, and supported browser tabs, including artwork and playback controls.

features.title: Built for the background
features.description: Aster can hide its Dock icon without closing its windows, reopen from Bar, resume Canvas shuffle, and launch at login after installation.

features.title: A Canvas that keeps moving
features.description: Timed shuffle supports still images, GIFs, and videos, remembers its state, and resumes after Aster reopens.

features.title: Updates that explain themselves
features.description: Aster can announce new releases, show exactly what changed, verify the download, and introduce new features after upgrading.
